---
description: "Oliver Frings — PhD candidate in economics at AgroParisTech and the Climate Economics Chair, Paris. Carbon market integrity and public preferences for climate and forest policy."
title: "Oliver Frings"
---

<!-- TODO: when a headshot exists, add it as assets/profile.jpg and place
     <img src="assets/profile.jpg" class="hero-photo"> floated right of the intro. -->

[Environmental economist · Pricing credibility in nature markets]{.lead-line}

## About

[I study whether nature markets deliver what they promise. On the demand side, I measure public support for forest conservation and carbon offsetting across Europe with discrete choice experiments. On the supply side, I price what a carbon credit actually delivers — net of leakage, storage duration, and reversal risk — with actuarial models and Monte Carlo simulation.]{.lead-line}

[Email](mailto:oliver.frings@agroparistech.fr){.pill}
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=DgFqKHYAAAAJ){.pill}
[ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-2682-2304){.pill}
[GitHub](https://github.com/o-frings){.pill}
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/oliver-frings-x161/){.pill}
[CV (PDF)](assets/CV_Oliver_Frings.pdf){.pill}

## Positions

[2023–present]{.when} [PhD Candidate in Economics]{.what} [[AgroParisTech](https://www.agroparistech.fr/) · Bureau d'Économie Théorique et Appliquée (BETA), Nancy — thesis *Pricing Credibility: Supply-Side Integrity and Public Acceptability in Nature Markets*, defence expected December 2026]{.meta}

[2025–present]{.when} [Research Fellow]{.what} [[Climate Economics Chair](https://www.chaireeconomieduclimat.org/en/), Paris]{.meta}

[2025–present]{.when} [Visiting Scholar]{.what} [Department of Land Economy ([C-EENRG](https://www.ceenrg.landecon.cam.ac.uk/)), University of Cambridge]{.meta}
